description: One realtime (openai-realtime) dictation turn as an ordered WIRE sequence: interleaved client events / streamed audio frames and the server's delta→delta→final echoes, ending in a bare fin. Unlike the sibling *.json fixtures (pure IntentEvent[] streams replayed through composeIntent), this captures the streaming CONTRACT — audio frames carry seg_1's PCM in seq order while talk-start/talk-end stay the segment boundaries; the partial deltas stream back WHILE the audio is still appending (before talk-end/commit — that's what makes the preview fill as you talk) and must NOT change the committed compose (only the final does). Each audio step's `bytes` pattern is expanded `repeat` times (PCM is repetitive; 3 × 4 bytes × 600 = 150 ms — a real utterance, comfortably past the 100 ms tap-discard minimum). Replayed by realtime.test.ts.
